nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Oh my! It's been a long time since we've had visitors here. Young friend, how did you come to this monastery?

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Transmigration? Well... that's quite unusual. But since you're here, make yourself at home. I can see you have excellent bone structure - perfect for learning Go!

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: In this world, Go mastery determines martial arts prowess. Since you're here, shall I teach you the fundamentals of Go?

options.text: I already know how to play Go
Master, what's the connection between Go and martial arts?
I'd like to hear more of your wisdom

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Ah, you don't understand yet! In this world, Go isn't just a pastime - it's the foundation of martial arts. The stronger your Go skills, the deeper your inner power.

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: All the masters in the martial world are Go masters as well. If you want to venture into the martial world, you must first master Go!

options.text: I see! Please teach me, Master

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Excellent! Excellent! Go requires great intuition. There's a saying: 'If you haven't become a master by 20, you never will.' Since you're here by fate, I'll teach you the mysteries of Go.

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Go is easy to learn but hard to master. The rules are simple - you'll understand in no time. Come, I'll explain as we play.

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: First, Go uses black and white stones on a board with 19 vertical and horizontal lines. Black moves first, and players alternate placing stones.

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Stones are placed on the intersections of the board. Once placed, they cannot be moved - only captured. This is 'no taking back moves' - like life itself, each step must be carefully considered!

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Each stone has 'liberties' - empty adjacent intersections. When a group of stones loses all its liberties, it's dead and must be captured.

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Come, let's play a 9x9 game. I'll let you go first. Slowly experience the mysteries of liberties and capturing.

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Excellent! Excellent! Though my Go skills aren't that high these years, my love for Go has never diminished. You played quite well for your first game.

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Next, I'll teach you about 'ko'. Sometimes both sides can capture each other, forming a cycle - this is ko fighting. The rule is you can't immediately recapture - you must play elsewhere first.

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Ko fighting is the most exquisite part of Go, often determining the outcome of a game. The proverb says: 'Seize ko threats first' - this is exactly what it means.

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Let's play another game. I'll deliberately create a ko situation to show you.

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Haha, you've already understood the power of ko fighting! When I was young, I lost many games because I didn't understand ko threats.

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Finally, let's discuss how to determine victory. Go is scored by 'points' - the larger your territory, the more points you have. At the end, count the points, and whoever has more wins.

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: When both sides don't want to play anymore, you can count territory. Remember: only living groups count as points - dead groups are captured and don't count.

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Alright, you've learned the basic rules. Let me play one more 19x19 game with you to see how well you've learned.

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Good! Excellent! Though you're a beginner, your intuition is outstanding. In this world, Go mastery determines martial prowess. Go out and explore, make friends through Go, and you'll understand the true essence.

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Remember: Go requires great intuition! There are many masters in the martial world - Hong Qigong, Linghu Chong, Guo Jing, Huang Rong... they're all first-class Go players.

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Go to Mount Hua and find Hong Qigong. His 'Mighty Dragon Repents' has its mysteries. Well, I need to continue studying Go records. Off you go!

options.text: Thank you for your teachings, Master!

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Oh? You already know Go? Good, then I won't be long-winded. Go to Mount Hua and find Hong Qigong - he has the way of true masters.

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Over the years, though my Go skills aren't brilliant, I've learned one thing: Go is like life - every move affects the overall situation, so don't act rashly.

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Also, keep a calm mind when playing. Don't be arrogant when winning, don't be discouraged when losing. I always brag about my Go skills, so I always lose, hahaha!

options.text: Thank you for your guidance, Master!

nodes.speaker: Taoist Musang
nodes.text: Go, go! I need to continue playing. If you have time, come back anytime for a few games!
